title: Domain-Specific Fine-Tuning of Large Language Models for HW/SW Interface Generation: An Empirical Study
data:
A Hassan, MS Saeed, B Senouci, B Benatallah- International Conference on, 2026
Developing hardware-software interfaces for cyber-physical and IoT systems 
remains a major bottleneck. While LLMs excel at code generation, they often 
produce non-compilable code and misuse peripherals due to limited hardware 
knowledge. In this work, we explore LLMs as complementary or alternative copilots 
to traditional design flow in Hardware-Software (HW/SW) systems design. To 
address this, we create a new dataset and then we propose targeted fine-tuning of

title: FASE: Fast Adaptive Semantic Entropy for Code Quality
data:
S Lin, L Tahvildari- arXiv preprint arXiv:2606.09800, 2026
Multi-agent code generation offers a promising paradigm for autonomous software 
development by simulating the human software engineering lifecycle. However, 
system reliability remains hindered by LLM hallucinations and error propagation 
across interacting agents. While semantic entropy provides a principled way to 
quantify uncertainty without ground-truth answers, current methods often rely on 
costly LLM-driven equivalence checks. In this work, we introduce Fast Adaptive
